summary: Full backend + frontend regression of the new GovCon Intelligence + org-join + capability-profile features. Backend: 12/12 new tests PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_intel_orgs.py (intel scan guard paths, fake-key job → error, SAMPLE report list+detail, add-to-pipeline + duplicate, join-code admin/rotate/RBAC, full register→join→duplicate flow, capability field persistence). Frontend: every requested data-testid renders and integrates correctly: Intelligence nav between Dashboard and Opportunities, SAMPLE intel-summary + intel-table (6 rows) + working search/filter/sort/export buttons (CSV and HTML files download), org-switcher → org-create-btn → create-org-modal and org-join-btn → join-org-modal both open, Admin Members tab join-code-card shows an 8-char code with Copy + Rotate (Rotate POSTs and updates the code + toast 'New join code generated'), Profile capability fields all editable and persist across reload.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking intel-history-btn yielded 0 elements matching [data-testid^="intel-history-item-"]. The dropdown opens (no JS error) but either uses a different test-id pattern or renders the items lazily. Non-blocking for the SAMPLE flow because the report is loaded by default. Recommend adding intel-history-item-{id} test-ids so the history list is automation-friendly.

integration_issues.flow: Intelligence → Add to pipeline UI toast
integration_issues.issue: intel-add-{idx} button click reached the backend (verified end-to-end in pytest add_then_duplicate test → pipeline grows by 1), but the sonner toast text was not captured by [role=status]/.toaster in the DOM scrape — likely portal-mounted late. Functionally OK, but adding a data-testid on the success toast would harden test signal.

issues: Rotate handler uses native window.confirm(), which is inconsistent with the rest of the modal-based UX (Modal component is already imported in Admin.js) and is harder to test/automate. Consider replacing with a Modal-based confirmation. Functionally correct — verified the POST /join-code/rotate fires and the displayed code updates once the dialog is accepted.

action_items: (Optional) Replace window.confirm in Admin.js JoinCodeCard.rotate with the existing Modal component for UX consistency and easier automation.
(Optional) Add data-testid='intel-history-item-{id}' on each item in the Intelligence history dropdown so QA can assert the list contents.
(Optional) Add a data-testid on the sonner success toast (or expose role='status') so UI tests can confirm 'Added to pipeline'.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/intel.py _execute_scan correctly normalizes Anthropic auth errors to 'Anthropic rejected the API key. Update it in Settings → API Keys.' — verified live with sk-ant-fake123.
routers/orgs.py join_org reactivates an inactive prior membership and 400s on a duplicate active membership — verified in test_join_full_flow.
routers/orgs.py _gen_join_code uses secrets.token_hex(4).upper() → exactly 8 hex chars; backend get/rotate both confirmed.
routers/intel.py add_to_pipeline correctly de-dupes on solNumber and skips the dedupe when solNumber is empty/'TBD' — verified with the last-row strategy in test_add_then_duplicate.
Admin.js JoinCodeCard.rotate is correct (setCode(data.joinCode) after POST), but the window.confirm gate makes it brittle to automate. UX nit only.
test_credentials: admin@govcon.io / Admin#2026 ; editor@govcon.io / Editor#2026 ; viewer@govcon.io / Editor#2026 (org id 6a23e286bc6ad92b27c235f0)
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created: (1) fake Anthropic key 'sk-ant-fake123' on org 6a23e286bc6ad92b27c235f0 secrets (per request — main agent to clear); (2) one freshly-registered viewer 'join_<uuid>@example.com' joined to the demo org via join code (visible in Admin → Members as 'Joiner'); (3) one opportunity added to the pipeline from the last index of the SAMPLE intel report (intentional, request allowed one). Profile capabilities field updated to 'UI-TEST cyber + cloud ...' from the UI test (other capability fields set to TEST values from the backend test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new features verified end-to-end. Notable real bug found: NONE. Only style/UX nits (window.confirm on rotate; missing test-ids on intel-history items and add-to-pipeline toast). The fake Anthropic key 'sk-ant-fake123' is still in db.secrets for org 6a23e286bc6ad92b27c235f0 and must be cleared by the main agent before any live scan attempts. Test artifacts left in DB: 1 'Joiner' member (random email) + 1 extra pipeline opportunity sourced from the sample report's last row + profile capability fields populated with TEST values. nav-intelligence sits between Dashboard and Opportunities (order: Dashboard, Intelligence, Opportunities, Company Profile, Admin, Settings). To test rotate via automation, register a page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) handler.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs reproduced. The transient 'Rotate did not change the code' observed in an early Playwright pass was a test-script issue: window.confirm() was auto-dismissed by Playwright (default behavior is to dismiss native dialogs), so the rotate handler returned early on `if (!window.confirm(...)) return;`. Once page.on('dialog', d.accept()) was registered, the POST /join-code/rotate fired, returned 200 with a new code, the toast appeared, and the displayed code updated as expected (8701B42D → C60DF785).
